title: Resolution of singularities for foliated threefolds in characteristic zero
abstract: We extend Hironaka-style resolution of singularities to algebraically integrable foliations on threefolds over fields of characteristic zero, settling a question raised by McQuillan.
ai_proof_review:
## Setup

The paper builds on the foliated MMP framework, lifting the threefold resolution proof to the foliated category. The technical heart is a foliated analogue of the maximal contact lemma.

## Lemma 4.2 (maximal contact)

The construction is direct: choose a hypersurface tangent to the foliation along the singular locus, then track the multiplicity drop under blowups. The induction on dimension is standard once the lemma is in hand. No gaps.

## Section 7 (termination)

The termination argument uses a foliated multiplicity invariant similar to Włodarczyk's. The decreasing chain condition is verified in detail; the only place that gave me pause was the handling of mixed singularities at flip loci, which the authors resolve with an explicit case split.

## Overall

A careful, complete proof. The result is what people in foliated birational geometry have been working toward for a decade; it unlocks several conditional papers.
